headline: White House AI crackdown opens door for China
detail: New AI export or regulatory restrictions could slow revenue growth for US chip and software names that depend on global sales. Your heaviest exposure - SMH, SOXL, CRWD - all have some sensitivity to AI policy sentiment. This headline adds an overhang, not a crisis yet.
rec: No trade triggered yet - watch how SMH reacts at the $637-634 scanner support zone. If it holds, no action needed. If it breaks, SMH and SOXL stops become critical.

headline: Warsh determined to slay inflation
detail: If Warsh signals a genuinely hawkish Fed posture, longer-duration growth stocks and high-multiple tech names get hit hardest - exactly your book. Rising rate expectations compress valuations on CRWD, ZS, PANW-type names. Offset slightly by headline 10 arguing inflation fears are overblown.
rec: No action now, but if 10-year yields spike meaningfully, consider trimming SOXL first since 3x leverage gets crushed fastest in a rate-scare selloff.

headline: AeroVironment skyrockets 21% on defense spending
detail: Defense drone spending surge is real and RTX is on the scanner as a Grade A setup (entry $187.43-187.91, stop $186.55). This confirms the defense theme has momentum today. Your current book has no direct defense exposure.
rec: RTX scanner entry $187.43-191.91, stop $186.55 is tight and clean. Defense tailwind from today's spending news is a real catalyst - small starter position worth considering if price pulls into that zone, given your margin exposure keep size small.

headline: Inflation fears overblown - what rate-hike camp gets wrong
detail: If this narrative takes hold, it removes a key headwind for high-multiple tech and semis - your entire book benefits. Lower rate anxiety means growth stocks can re-rate higher and margin traders get breathing room.
rec: No new buy needed - this is a tailwind for what you already own. If CRWD or SMH pull back into their scanner zones today, adding to existing positions makes more sense than opening new ones.
